input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #5851 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.lucene:lucene-core:6.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Many Lucene getting-started examples use companion modules such as lucene-analyzers-common or lucene-queryparser, but those are not required to exercise lucene-core itself and would broaden the test scope.

Tests that intentionally exercise MMapDirectory unmapping or deep JDK internals may need to preserve the existing JVM add-opens configuration already present in the current scaffold, but this is not a new deterministic setup requirement for reaching core coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No setup beyond the existing/generated lucene-core test scaffold is concretely required. The resolved org.apache.lucene:lucene-core:6.0.0 POM declares no library dependencies, the jar contains its own built-in SPI service descriptors for core codecs/formats, and meaningful coverage can be reached through core APIs such as attributes, storage, codecs/SPI loading, document fields, indexing primitives, and utility classes without Docker or external services. The reported javac failure is an API change: AttributeImpl.reflectWith(AttributeReflector) is abstract in 6.0.0, so the existing test implementation must be updated rather than adding setup.
